diseases.name: Healthy
diseases.symptoms: No visible symptoms. Leaves are green, firm, and free from spots or discoloration.
diseases.description: The plant is healthy with no signs of disease or pest damage.

diseases.name: Early Blight
diseases.symptoms: Dark brown to black concentric rings (target-like spots) on older leaves. Yellowing around spots. Leaves may curl and drop.
diseases.description: Caused by Alternaria solani fungus. Common in warm, humid conditions. Spreads through wind, rain splash, and infected plant debris.
diseases.severity_info: Can cause 20-50% yield loss if untreated. Early detection is critical.

diseases.name: Late Blight
diseases.symptoms: Water-soaked, pale green to dark brown lesions on leaves. White mold growth on leaf undersides. Rapid wilting.
diseases.description: Caused by Phytophthora infestans. Thrives in cool, wet weather. Extremely destructive — can destroy entire crops in days.
diseases.severity_info: Can cause 100% yield loss. Immediate action required upon detection.

diseases.name: Bacterial Spot
diseases.symptoms: Small, water-soaked spots on leaves that turn brown/black. Spots may have yellow halos. Fruit may develop raised, scab-like lesions.
diseases.description: Caused by Xanthomonas bacteria. Spreads through contaminated seeds, rain splash, and overhead irrigation.
diseases.severity_info: Can reduce fruit quality and yield by 10-50%.

diseases.name: Leaf Mold
diseases.symptoms: Pale green to yellowish spots on upper leaf surface. Olive-green to brown velvety mold on leaf undersides.
diseases.description: Caused by Passalora fulva (formerly Cladosporium fulvum). Favored by high humidity (>85%) and moderate temperatures.
diseases.severity_info: Reduces photosynthesis and can cause 10-30% yield loss.

diseases.name: Mosaic Virus
diseases.symptoms: Mottled light and dark green patterns on leaves. Leaf distortion, curling, and reduced size. Stunted plant growth.
diseases.description: Viral infection spread by aphids, whiteflies, and contaminated tools. No cure exists — management focuses on prevention.
diseases.severity_info: Can cause 20-70% yield loss depending on infection timing.

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.symptoms: White, powdery fungal growth on leaf surfaces. Leaves may yellow, curl, and eventually drop.
diseases.description: Caused by various Erysiphales fungi. Favored by warm days and cool nights with moderate humidity.
diseases.severity_info: Can reduce yield by 10-40% depending on severity and crop stage.

diseases.name: Rust
diseases.symptoms: Orange-brown to reddish-brown pustules on leaves and stems. Pustules release powdery spores when touched.
diseases.description: Caused by Puccinia fungi. Spreads rapidly through wind-borne spores. Favored by moderate temperatures and high humidity.
diseases.severity_info: Can cause 10-70% yield loss. Epidemics can be devastating.

diseases.name: Anthracnose
diseases.symptoms: Dark, sunken lesions on fruits, leaves, and stems. Lesions may have concentric rings. Pink/orange spore masses in wet conditions.
diseases.description: Caused by Colletotrichum fungi. Spread by rain splash and wind. Common in warm, humid tropical conditions.
diseases.severity_info: Can cause 10-80% post-harvest losses in fruits.

diseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
diseases.symptoms: Sudden wilting of entire plant or individual branches. No yellowing before wilting. Brown discoloration of vascular tissue.
diseases.description: Caused by Ralstonia solanacearum. Soil-borne pathogen that enters through wounds. Thrives in warm, wet soils.
diseases.severity_info: Can cause 100% loss in susceptible varieties. Very difficult to control once established.
